title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
This World Savings Day, Save Some More with These Products!
Ever heard the saying – The fruits of hard work are always sweet? The same thing goes for savings.
When you undertake to save regularly, it might feel like a challenge. However, a disciplined approach would help you accumulate a corpus sufficient enough to meet your expenses and even fulfil your goal.
Warren Buffet says, ‘Don’t save what is left after spending, but spend what is left after saving’. Investment guru sums up the importance of saving in one simple line! If he believes that saving is important, how can we refuse!!
World Savings Day is on 30th October 2022. The day emphasizes the need to save and creates awareness among people. This time, why don’t you get financially empowered?
In your financial empowerment journey, merely saving is not enough. You need to invest too. So, here are some products that you choose to save and create a corpus for your future goals –
Term plan for emergency planning
When saving towards a corpus for your financial goals, do not overlook emergency planning. Life has a way of disrupting even the ideal laid plans. As such, you need to make provisions for emergencies so that you can protect your savings from being drained out.
When it comes to emergency planning, a term plan comes into the picture. The plan covers the risk of premature death. It promises a guaranteed* benefit on death during the policy tenure. So, when you invest in a term life insurance policy, you can buy financial security against premature demise. You can ensure that your family receives financial support when you are not around so that they can meet their financial needs.
Child plan for child planning
This is applicable to parents who want to plan for their child’s bright future. Child insurance plans help in creating a secured corpus for the child. These plans come with a premium waiver benefit that ensures the continuity of savings even when the parent is not around. Even after the parent’s death, the child's plan creates the corpus and secures the child’s future.
While there are other investment avenues that you can choose for child planning, a child plan trumps them with its premium waiver benefit. It ensures continued savings, even after the parent’s demise.
Equity for attractive returns
You should save on avenues that can generate attractive returns. As experts say, make your money work for you.
To do that, equity is a good asset class that you can choose. Equity has the potential to generate attractive returns on investments. You can choose from equity stocks, equity mutual funds and even ULIPs that offer equity funds for investment.
Though volatile, equity can yield good returns if you give your investment time and save in a disciplined manner.
Debt for stability
While equity can deliver attractive returns, you need to add stability to your portfolio to protect it against sudden volatility in the market. Moreover, portfolio diversification is an important tenet when saving and investing.
This is where debt instruments come into the picture. Debt instruments are fixed-income instruments that have low volatility risks. As such, they can generate stable returns on your investments.
To invest in debt, there are multiple options that you can choose from. These include fixed deposits, Public Provident Fund (PPF), National Savings Certificate (NSC), debt mutual funds, life insurance endowment and money back plans, debt funds under ULIPs, etc.
With a plethora of products available in the market, you can save and make your savings generate returns too. However, when saving, here are a few things to remember –
Identify and prioritize your financial goals. You need to know what you are saving and when would you need the saving.
Estimate the corpus needed for each goal. This would give you an idea of how much to save.
Consider inflation when estimating the saving amount. Try and step up your savings regularly so that the corpus keeps pace with the rising costs.
Assess your risk appetite too. Different saving products have different risk profiles. Choose those avenues that align with your risk tolerance.
Assess the tax implication of the avenues where you save. The Income Tax Act, 19611 has listed various avenues that can give you tax benefits. Pick such avenues to build a corpus and save taxes too. For instance, a life insurance policy can help you save and also reduce your tax liability.
The bottom line
While World Savings Day can endeavour to create awareness and nudge you to save for your goals, it would matter only if you act on it. So, this World Savings Day, let’s make a difference. Start saving first, spending later. If you are already saving, assess the adequacy of the same. Explore the different investment avenues and pick those that are aimed at your financial goals and can also help you save tax. Take hints from the aforementioned products and save away for a financially secure future.
